# UC-22.6.20 · Remote Working — VPN Split Tunnel and Sensitive App Access

## Description

Detects VPN sessions advertising split tunnel with subsequent access to finance or HR apps from same user session.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes proxy, vpn and sourcetypes bluecoat:proxy, globalprotect. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize user identifiers; (2) tune URL patterns; (3) align with remote work policy; (4) user education. Indexes required: `index=proxy`, `index=vpn`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=bluecoat:proxy`, `sourcetype=globalprotect`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Palo Alto GlobalProtect or AnyConnect logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `globalprotect` or `vpn:session`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize user identifiers; (2) tune URL patterns; (3) align with remote work policy; (4) user education.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype=globalprotect earliest=-24h
| eval split=if(match(config,"split"),1,0)
| stats max(split) as used_split by user, private_ip
| join user [| search index=proxy sourcetype=bluecoat:proxy earliest=-24h | stats values(url) as urls by user | where match(urls,"(?i)/finance/|/hr/") ]
| where used_split=1
```

Understanding this SPL

**Remote Working — VPN Split Tunnel and Sensitive App Access (A.6.7)** — Detects VPN sessions advertising split tunnel with subsequent access to finance or HR apps from same user session.

Documented **Data sources**: `globalprotect` or `vpn:session`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto GlobalProtect or AnyConnect logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vpn; **sourcetype**: globalprotect. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vpn, sourcetype=globalprotect, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **split** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **user, private_ip**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where used_split=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Map

## SPL

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype=globalprotect earliest=-24h
| eval split=if(match(config,"split"),1,0)
| stats max(split) as used_split by user, private_ip
| join user [| search index=proxy sourcetype=bluecoat:proxy earliest=-24h | stats values(url) as urls by user | where match(urls,"(?i)/finance/|/hr/") ]
| where used_split=1
```

## Visualization

Table, Map. Key fields: `private_ip`, `split`, `urls`, `used_split`, `user`.
